The teacher delivers an interactive 75-minute lesson that begins with a video clip of a Premier League footballer kicking a curling free kick, followed by a real-time simulation using a PhET interactive tool. Students then work in small groups to design “careers profiles” of three STEM professionals whose work relies on understanding forces: a Formula 1 aerodynamicist, a civil engineer designing suspension bridges, and a sports scientist optimizing athlete biomechanics. Each group must identify the curriculum link (e.g., F=ma, drag force) and present their findings using a template developed with a local STEM Ambassador.
